name: All Payday Loans
description_short: Online loan marketplace connecting borrowers with a network of lenders offering payday loans, cash advances, and bad credit loans from $500 to $5,000.
description_long:
All Payday Loans operates pdadvanceloans.com, an online lead-generation marketplace that matches consumers with a network of third-party lenders. The site is not a direct lender — it collects borrower information via a single form and routes that data to participating lenders who then compete to extend offers. The company's focus is on speed and accessibility, positioning itself as a connector for people who need fast cash and may not qualify through traditional channels.

The platform serves four core loan types: bad credit loans, payday loans, personal loans, and cash advances. The primary form advertises loan amounts between $1,000 and $5,000, while a secondary section references the lender network's broader range up to $35,000. Borrowers fill out one online form, can receive up to five loan offers, and may receive funds deposited directly to a bank account — sometimes within 24 hours of approval. The site claims loan offers can arrive in as little as 15 minutes.

One distinguishing feature is the site's own consumer-protection language. The homepage prominently warns visitors that requesting advance fees is a scam indicator, and explicitly states that payday loans 'should be last resort.' This kind of disclosure on a payday loan marketplace is relatively uncommon and suggests some awareness of consumer risk. The site also references a 3% figure with no accompanying explanation — it is unclear whether this refers to a rate, fee, or is a partial disclosure fragment.

Honestly, this is a thin lead-generation website with minimal transparency. There is no information about the company behind the marketplace, no disclosed lender partners, no APR range, and no licensing details. The 3% figure is unexplained and potentially misleading. Consumers who submit their information are immediately redirected to a longer form, and the actual loan terms are entirely controlled by third-party lenders in an undisclosed network. This is a functional tool for borrowers who want multiple offers quickly, but it offers very little basis for informed comparison before submitting personal data.
address: 13158 Spring Hill Dr, Spring Hill, FL 34609

company_info.headquarters: 13158 Spring Hill Dr, Spring Hill, FL 34609

pros: Single form submission connects borrowers with up to five lender offers
Explicitly serves bad credit borrowers — not limited to prime credit
Fast process: loan offer potentially in as little as 15 minutes
Funding can arrive via direct bank deposit, sometimes within 24 hours
Covers multiple loan types: payday, cash advance, personal loans, bad credit
Homepage prominently warns users that advance-fee requests are scams
Site itself advises that payday loans should be a last resort — rare honesty for this segment
cons: Not a direct lender — actual rates, fees, and terms are set by undisclosed third-party lenders
No APR range disclosed; the lone '3%' figure on the homepage is unexplained and potentially misleading
No company information, licensing details, or lender partner names provided
Submitting the initial form redirects to a second, more detailed form — the process is not as simple as advertised
Payday and cash advance products in this segment typically carry very high APRs — site warns of this but provides no specifics
best_for: Borrowers with bad or no credit who need fast access to $500–$5,000
Consumers who want to receive multiple lender offers from a single application
People facing a short-term cash shortfall who have exhausted lower-cost options
Borrowers who prioritize speed of funding over loan cost
diagnosis: This site is best suited for bad-credit borrowers who need cash quickly and want to cast a wide net across multiple lenders with one form submission. The main caveat is that it is a lead-generation marketplace with no disclosed lender partners, no APR transparency, and terms entirely determined by third parties — borrowers should read all lender disclosures carefully before accepting any offer.
services: Personal loan matching (up to $5,000 via form; up to $35,000 via network)
Multi-lender comparison (up to 5 offers per application)
Loan offer delivery in as fast as 15 minutes
Same- or next-day funding (sometimes within 24 hours)
Scam awareness disclosures for advance-fee fraud
